description: ADR 095 — unified observability: one Grafana pane over the centralized stores. TELEMETRY (traces/metrics/logs) lives in ClickHouse (the OTel collector's clickhouse exporter); the BUSINESS OF RECORD (runs, cost ledger, governance) lives in Postgres. The two are correlated by trace_id (ADR 095 D4) — the cross-store table joins the authoritative cost/outcome (Postgres) with high-cardinality span volume (ClickHouse). Prototype for the unified-observability migration (ADR 095 D6 steps 1-2).

options.content:
## One pane, two centralized stores

**Telemetry → ClickHouse** (traces + metrics + logs, written by the OTel collector's `clickhouse` exporter). **Business of record → Postgres** (`workflow_runs`, cost ledger, governance — authoritative + transactional). The panels below read **both datasources in one dashboard**, and the bottom table is the **cross-store join on `trace_id`** — the authoritative cost/outcome from Postgres alongside the span volume from ClickHouse.

**Centralizing principle (ADR 095 D1):** the OTel collector is the single ingestion hub; Grafana is the single query pane. Each signal keeps a fit-for-purpose store — we do NOT force traces into Postgres. `trace_id` (D4) is what makes this *unified*, not merely co-located.

_Local demo note:_ the cross-store join lights up with real correlated traffic (a run stamps the same `trace_id` on its Postgres run record AND its ClickHouse spans). With only the seeded Postgres rows, the Postgres panels populate and the ClickHouse panels fill as OTLP arrives.

panels.description: Authoritative spend from the business of record (Postgres). Source: workflow_runs.cost_usd.

targets.rawSql: SELECT workflow, tier, sum(cost_usd) AS cost_usd FROM workflow_runs GROUP BY workflow, tier ORDER BY cost_usd DESC;

panels.title: Run cost by workflow / tier (Postgres · business of record)

panels.description: Workflow outcomes from the business of record. Source: workflow_runs.status.

targets.rawSql: SELECT status, count(*) AS runs FROM workflow_runs GROUP BY status ORDER BY runs DESC;

panels.title: Runs by status (Postgres)

panels.description: Governance decisions from the audit store (Postgres). Source: governance_decisions.

targets.rawSql: SELECT effect, count(*) AS decisions FROM governance_decisions GROUP BY effect ORDER BY decisions DESC;

panels.title: Governance decisions by effect (Postgres)

panels.description: Span ingest volume from the unified telemetry store (ClickHouse). Source: otel.otel_traces — written by the collector's clickhouse exporter.

targets.rawSql: SELECT toStartOfMinute(Timestamp) AS time, ServiceName AS service, count() AS spans FROM otel.otel_traces WHERE $__timeFilter(Timestamp) GROUP BY time, service ORDER BY time

panels.title: Span volume by service (ClickHouse · telemetry)

panels.description: Slowest recent traces from the unified telemetry store (ClickHouse). Source: otel.otel_traces aggregated by TraceId.

targets.rawSql: SELECT TraceId AS trace_id, any(ServiceName) AS service, count() AS spans, max(Duration) AS duration FROM otel.otel_traces WHERE $__timeFilter(Timestamp) GROUP BY TraceId ORDER BY duration DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Top traces by duration (ClickHouse)

panels.description: THE cross-store pivot (ADR 095 D4): each workflow run's authoritative cost/outcome from Postgres, JOINED on trace_id with its span volume from ClickHouse. Query A = Postgres (business of record); Query B = ClickHouse (telemetry); joined by the `trace_id` field transformation. Populates fully once real runs flow (the run's trace_id matches its spans).

targets.rawSql: SELECT trace_id, workflow, tier, status, cost_usd FROM workflow_runs ORDER BY created_at DESC;
SELECT TraceId AS trace_id, count() AS spans, max(Duration) AS duration_ns FROM otel.otel_traces GROUP BY TraceId

panels.title: Cross-store: run cost/outcome (Postgres) ⨯ span volume (ClickHouse), joined on trace_id

title: mdk - unified observability
